---
title: "Identity Modernization — Case Study"
subtitle: "Identity plane governance during a large-scale directory migration"
doc-type: case-study-pattern
audience: [Customer, Sponsor, Program Lead]
classification: Public
boundary: GCC-Moderate
status: framing-pattern
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/whitepapers/modernization-governance-whitepaper.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/architecture-series/aodim-architecture.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/modernization-specs/identity/identity.qmd"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# Identity Modernization — Case Study

::: {.callout-important}
## Framing pattern, not a fabricated engagement
This page is the **canonical engagement pattern** for an identity-
modernization case study, structured so that real engagement material
can be anchored on top when it becomes externally releasable.
Specific metrics, agency identifiers, and timelines are intentionally
absent until real material is reviewed.
:::

![Identity migration engagement pattern diagram](images/identity-modernization-case-stud-image-01-identity-migration-engagement-pa.png){#fig-identity-modernization-case-study-image-01 fig-alt="Five-phase horizontal sequence (Canon-First → Modernization Adapters → Drift-Driven Cleanup pre-cutover → Cutover → Post-Cutover). Each phase shows the substrate's role: canon authoring, adapter registration, drift findings remediation, identity-plane source switch, continuous remediation. Above the sequence, a \"Substrate behaviors\" panel lists the four metrics a real engagement would document (provenance breakage rate, attribute-quality drift, cross-pillar correlation health, AODIM realization). Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic." width="85%"}

## Engagement archetype

A federal agency operating Active Directory at scale (>100k identities,
multi-forest topology, decade-old OU hierarchy) plans transition to
Entra ID under GCC-Moderate. The program has identified the technical
migration tooling but has not addressed the **architectural inversion**
from hierarchical placement to attribute-driven access (see
[Modernization Governance Whitepaper](../whitepapers/modernization-governance-whitepaper.qmd)).

Without a substrate, the program risks inheriting the OU-driven model
in Entra ID and losing most cloud-native value.

## Starting state (typical)

- AD forest topology with several thousand OUs.
- Group memberships authored manually; movers handled via ticket-
  driven re-permissioning.
- Identity claims consumed inconsistently across SIEM, IDP, IPAM,
  and ATO documentation.
- ATO package describes intended controls; substrate truth at any
  given moment is not reproducibly verifiable.

## UIAO intervention pattern

### Phase 1 — canon-first

Author the AODIM attribute model in canon:

- Canonical attribute set (`assignment_scope`, `organizational_role`,
  `classification_clearance`).
- Computed-group rules per attribute combinations.
- Provenance envelope per identity claim.

Substrate walker passes against the new canon before any runtime
change.

### Phase 2 — modernization adapters

Stand up `entra-id`, `active-directory`, `entra-policy-targeting`,
and the relevant attribute-source adapters
(`piv-usaccess`, agency-specific HR adapter). Adapters emit canonical
claims with full provenance from day one — including during the
parallel-running period.

### Phase 3 — drift-driven cleanup pre-cutover

Pre-cutover runs surface inherited inconsistencies (stale attributes,
group rules with retired references, broken provenance chains) as
canonical drift findings. Each is remediated under the canonical
SLA tempo.

### Phase 4 — cutover

The directory transition is a substrate event: the identity plane's
authoritative source switches from on-prem AD to Entra ID. Canon is
already in place to interpret post-cutover claims.

### Phase 5 — post-cutover

Drift findings continue to surface inconsistencies; auto-remediation
handles deterministic fixes; the canon-change ADR process handles
residual model evolution.

## Substrate behaviors observed (pattern)

A real engagement would document, with metrics, behaviors such as:

- **Provenance breakage rate during cutover** — how many claims
  emitted with incomplete chains during parallel-run, attributable to
  which adapter, remediated within which SLA tier.
- **Attribute-quality drift** — how many identities passed canonical
  validation pre-cutover vs. post-cutover, and how the gap closed
  under drift-driven cleanup.
- **Cross-pillar correlation health** — whether the same identity
  resolves identically across PIV, Entra, and HR systems post-
  cutover.
- **AODIM realization** — what fraction of access decisions are
  attribute-computed vs. manually-administered post-cutover.

These metrics are the credible-claim surface for an externally
releasable case study. The substrate produces them deterministically;
authoring requires real engagement material.

## Lessons that propagate back into canon

A real engagement typically reveals:

- Edge cases in the AODIM attribute model that require canon-change
  ADRs.
- Adapter conformance gaps (where a vendor's API does not cleanly map
  to the canonical claim shape).
- Operational tempo tuning (SLA tiers that work in theory but require
  agency-specific staffing).

Each lesson lands as either an ADR, a canon-document update, or an
adapter-registry change. The case study, when complete, becomes a
provenance source for those changes.

## When this study can be promoted to "real engagement"

Per the section's authoring posture, promotion requires:

1. Engagement material reviewed and cleared for external release.
2. Specific metrics anonymized or formally authorized for citation.
3. Canon-change traceability — every "lesson" cited points to the
   ADR or canon-document update it produced.
4. Sponsor-side review and approval of the narrative.

Until then, this page documents the engagement *pattern* a customer
should expect, not a specific engagement outcome.

## Related documents

- [Whitepaper — Modernization Governance](../whitepapers/modernization-governance-whitepaper.qmd)
- [Architecture Series — AODIM](../architecture-series/aodim-architecture.qmd)
- [Modernization Spec — Identity](../modernization-specs/identity/identity.qmd)
- [ADR-008: Zero Trust Identity](../../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md)
